_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder

title: Network Basics
subtitle: connecting the digital world!
overview: This session introduces students to the basics of computer networks, how devices communicate, and how the internet works. Students explore concepts like LAN, WAN, routers, Wi-Fi, IP addresses, and data transfer through fun activities and real-world examples.

images._note: Add photos from networking demos, router setup activities, cable identification, and internet communication examples.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of network diagram or classroom activity
gallery.caption: Networks connect devices to share information
Routers help devices communicate with the internet

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students building a small network
gallery.caption: LAN connects devices in a small area
The internet is a giant network of networks

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Ethernet cable and connector photo
gallery.caption: Ethernet cables transfer data between devices
Servers provide data and services to client devices

keyTopics: What is a Network? — Devices connected to share data
LAN (Local Area Network) — Small networks like schools or homes
WAN (Wide Area Network) — Large networks across cities and countries
Internet — A global network connecting billions of devices
Router — Directs data traffic between devices
IP Address — Unique identity of a device on a network
Server & Client — Devices that provide and receive information

youtubeVideos.title: What is Wi-Fi?
youtubeVideos.desc: How wireless communication works in daily life.

activity.title: Build a Human Network
steps: Assign students as computers, routers, and servers
Connect students using string to represent network cables
Show what happens when a connection breaks
Discuss how the internet connects the world

quiz.explanation: A network connects devices so they can communicate and share data.
LAN means Local Area Network — used in homes, schools, and offices.
WAN connects devices across large distances like cities or countries.

quiz.q: What device connects your home network to the internet?
quiz.explanation: Routers manage and direct internet traffic between devices.
Wi-Fi allows devices to connect wirelessly to a network.

options: A global network of connected devices
quiz.explanation: The internet is a worldwide system of interconnected networks.

options: A unique identity for a device on a network
quiz.explanation: Every device on a network has a unique IP address.
Servers provide information and services to other devices called clients.

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a LAN?
quiz.explanation: A school computer lab is a small local network.

options: They help devices communicate and share information
quiz.explanation: Networks allow devices to exchange information quickly and efficiently.
